what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG line icon depicting a lightbulb, using currentColor strokes and no fill.
observations: The root svg has viewBox="0 0 24 24" with explicit width and height of 24.
The icon uses fill="none" and stroke="currentColor" so color is inherited from context.
Stroke styling is set to 1.5 width with round line caps and joins.
The drawing is composed of two path elements: one for the bulb base and one for the bulb outline and neck transition.
standouts: Uses round stroke treatment for a soft outline icon style.
conventions: Keeps styling as root-level SVG attributes inherited by child paths.
Uses no ids, classes, embedded styles, scripts, or external references.
concerns: No title, desc, or aria attributes are included for standalone accessibility context.
notes: A small inline-ready lightbulb outline icon with inherited color and fixed 24px dimensions.
questions: Should this icon be treated as decorative or given accessible labeling by the consuming code?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename-implied purpose matches the content: it is a lightbulb SVG icon.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable lightbulb symbol that can inherit surrounding text or UI color while remaining visually consistent at 24px.
contribution: Contributes a compact currentColor lightbulb glyph that a fused system would keep as an inherited-color idea or insight icon asset.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a 24x24 stroke-only SVG lightbulb icon made from two path elements with inherited currentColor styling.
intention_for_embedding: It was for representing an idea, insight, or lightbulb concept as a reusable UI icon asset.
